description_long: This is a medieval-style longsword with a straight, double-edged blade that tapers to a sharp point. The blade appears to be forged from dark metal, exhibiting a subtle linear texture along its length. The crossguard is simple and straight, providing hand protection, while the handle is cylindrical and ends in a faceted, polygonal pommel. The entire sword has a uniform dark coloration, suggesting it is made from a single type of metal or finished with a dark coating. There are no visible engravings, inlays, or decorative elements on the blade, guard, or handle. The sword is robust and functional, designed for combat or ceremonial display.
description: A dark, straight-bladed longsword with a simple crossguard and faceted pommel, lacking ornamentation and finished in a uniform dark metal.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the straight blade, crossguard, and symmetrical handle with a polygonal pommel.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the thinness of the blade and the straight alignment of the handle and guard.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the blade, crossguard, and the faceted pommel, mirroring the front.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again emphasizing the blade's thinness and the straight, unadorned handle.
attribute_handle_design: straight, cylindrical, faceted pommel, dark metal, no visible grip texture
